text.title: WCAG 2.2 Compliance Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding wcag-22-compliance. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Conformance report MUST cover all 9 new SC: 2.4.11, 2.4.12, 2.4.13, 2.5.7, 2.5.8, 3.2.6, 3.3.7, 3.3.8, 3.3.9. Partial coverage is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Claiming WCAG 2.2 AA without covering every new SC is non-conformance and exposes legal risk.

rule.statement: Per 2.4.11 + 2.4.13, focus indicators MUST be visible AND not obscured by overlapping content (e.g. sticky headers, cookie banners).
rule.rationale: Sticky elements clipping focus is the most common 2.4.11 violation; visual focus is a load-bearing keyboard-nav signal.

rule.statement: Per 2.5.8, all interactive targets MUST be ≥24×24 CSS px unless exception applies (inline links, spacing >24px around the target).
rule.rationale: Sub-24px targets fail motor accessibility; 24px is the floor, 44px recommended for primary actions.

rule.statement: Per 3.3.8, authentication MUST NOT require a cognitive function test (memorize, transcribe) unless an alternative exists. Captchas of the puzzle type are forbidden as sole auth.
rule.rationale: Cognitive-function auth excludes users with cognitive disabilities; alternatives like passkeys or magic links satisfy.

rule.statement: Per 3.2.6, help mechanisms (contact link, FAQ link) MUST appear in the same relative order across pages when present.
rule.rationale: Inconsistent help placement creates cognitive load disproportionately for users with memory/attention disabilities.
